[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Formal title indicating the work is officially sanctioned and included in the Four Treasuries.
  • 子部十二 - Refers to the twelfth category in the classification of the Four Treasuries, which includes various types of literature and texts.
  • 杂事之属 - Refers to a subcategory of miscellaneous events or anecdotes.
  • 提要 - A summary or introduction to the work.
  • 不着撰人名氏 - Indicates that the author's name is unknown.
  • 记唐初至元和中杂事 - Means 'records various events from the early Tang Dynasty to the reign of Emperor Yuanhe.'
  • 唐宋艺文志俱不载 - Indicates that the work is not listed in the bibliographies of the Tang and Song Dynasties.
  • 前有自序 - Refers to the preface written by the author.
  • 八年夏南行岭峤 - Refers to the year 8, summer, when the author traveled south to the mountainous regions.
  • 考穆宗以后惟太和大中咸通乃有八年 - Refers to the fact that the reign of Emperor Mu only had an eighth year, and the subsequent reigns of Emperor Taizong, Emperor Dazong, and Emperor Xiantong also had an eighth year.
  • 此书既不着其纪元之号 - Indicates that the book does not specify the era name.
  • 所云八年者亦不知其在何时也 - Means 'it is also unclear when the eighth year refers to.'
  • 所录唐公卿事迹言论颇详 - Means 'the records of the deeds and words of Tang nobles and officials are quite detailed.'
  • 多为史所采用 - Means 'many of these records are used in historical works.'
  • 间及于诙谐谈谑及朝野琐事 - Refers to the inclusion of humorous anecdotes and trivial matters from court and common life.
  • 往往与他说部相出入 - Means 'often differ from other works.'
  • 称贞元中郑国韩国二公主加谥为公主追谥之始 - Refers to the posthumous titles given to Princesses Zhengguo and Haunguo during the reign of Emperor Chengyuan.
  • 据博异记乃巨盗而此纪之以为异人 - Means 'according to the *Bo Yi Ji* (Book of Strange Stories), the old man was actually a notorious thief, but here he is recorded as a strange person.'
  • 要其可据者实十之六七 - Means 'but the parts that can be relied upon are about six or seven out of ten.'
  • 乾隆四十四年六月恭校上 - Refers to the date and the act of submitting the work for official review during the reign of Emperor Qianlong.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - A major Chinese encyclopedia compiled during the Qing Dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - One of the ten categories in the Four Treasuries, which includes various types of literature.
  • 杂事之属 - A subcategory of miscellaneous events or anecdotes.
  • 提要 - A summary or introduction to the work.
  • 不着撰人名氏 - Indicates that the author's name is unknown.
  • 记唐初至元和中杂事 - Refers to the early Tang Dynasty and the reign of Emperor Yuanhe.
  • 唐宋艺文志俱不载 - Indicates that the work is not listed in the bibliographies of the Tang and Song Dynasties.
  • 前有自序 - Refers to the preface written by the author.
  • 八年夏南行岭峤 - Refers to the year 8, summer, when the author traveled south to the mountainous regions.
  • 考穆宗以后惟太和大中咸通乃有八年 - Refers to the reigns of Emperors Mu, Taizong, Dazong, and Xiantong.
  • 此书既不着其纪元之号 - Indicates that the book does not specify the era name.
  • 所云八年者亦不知其在何时也 - Means 'it is also unclear when the eighth year refers to.'
  • 所录唐公卿事迹言论颇详 - Means 'the records of the deeds and words of Tang nobles and officials are quite detailed.'
  • 多为史所采用 - Means 'many of these records are used in historical works.'
  • 间及于诙谐谈谑及朝野琐事 - Refers to the inclusion of humorous anecdotes and trivial matters from court and common life.
  • 往往与他说部相出入 - Means 'often differ from other works.'
  • 称贞元中郑国韩国二公主加谥为公主追谥之始 - Refers to the posthumous titles given to Princesses Zhengguo and Haunguo during the reign of Emperor Chengyuan.
  • 据博异记乃巨盗而此纪之以为异人 - Means 'according to the *Bo Yi Ji* (Book of Strange Stories), the old man was actually a notorious thief, but here he is recorded as a strange person.'
  • 要其可据者实十之六七 - Means 'but the parts that can be relied upon are about six or seven out of ten.'
  • 乾隆四十四年六月恭校上 - Refers to the date and the act of submitting the work for official review during the reign of Emperor Qianlong.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 總纂官【臣】紀昀【臣】陸錫熊【臣】孫士毅 - Indicates the editors of the work, with '【臣】' marking the editors as officials.
  • 總校官【臣】陸費墀 - Indicates the chief proofreader, also marked as an official.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - Refers to the 'Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature,' an imperial compilation project.
  • 不有博奕者乎猶賢乎已 - A quote from the *Book of Rites* (禮記), meaning 'Is there not something like chess or dice? It is better than idleness.'
  • 吾不試故藝 - From the *Analects* (論語), meaning 'I do not practice, hence I am not skilled.'
  • 清俸買來手自校 - Means 'purchased with my salary and personally proofread.'
  • 鬻及借人為不孝 - Means 'selling or lending them out is unrighteous.'
  • 陽道州城之為朝士也 - Means 'Yang Daozhou, when he was a court official.'
  • 家苦貧 - Means 'his family was very poor.'
  • 質錢 - Means 'to pawn' or 'to pledge.'
  • 費縣西漏澤 - Refers to a marshy area west of Feixian.
  • 漫十數里 - Means 'spread over tens of miles.'
  • 歲時雨降即泛溢自滿 - Means 'when it rains, it overflows and fills up.'
  • 蒲魚之利 - Means 'the benefits of raising fish and water plants.'
  • 人實賴焉 - Means 'people really depend on it.'
  • 白露應節 - Means 'in response to the White Dew solar term.'
  • 一夕即一空如掃 - Means 'it empties out in one night, like being swept clean.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 總纂官【臣】紀昀【臣】陸錫熊【臣】孫士毅 - The editors of the *Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature*, a major imperial compilation project during the Qing dynasty.
  • 總校官【臣】陸費墀 - The chief proofreader of the same project.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - The 'Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature,' a massive collection of Chinese literature and scholarship commissioned by the Qianlong Emperor.
  • 不有博奕者乎猶賢乎已 - A quote from the *Book of Rites*, emphasizing the value of engaging in activities to avoid idleness.
  • 吾不試故藝 - A quote from the *Analects*, highlighting the importance of practice in developing skills.
  • 清俸買來手自校 - A personal statement about the effort put into acquiring and proofreading books.
  • 鬻及借人為不孝 - A moral statement about the proper treatment of books.
  • 陽道州城之為朝士也 - Refers to Yang Daozhou, a historical figure who served as a court official.
  • 費縣西漏澤 - A specific geographical location in Shandong province, known for its marshy landscape.

Proper Nouns

  • 總纂官 - Chief editor.
  • 【臣】紀昀 - Ji Yun, one of the chief editors.
  • 【臣】陸錫熊 - Lu Xixiong, another chief editor.
  • 【臣】孫士毅 - Sun Shiyi, another chief editor.
  • 總校官 - Chief proofreader.
  • 【臣】陸費墀 - Lu Feicheng, the chief proofreader.
  • 欽定四庫全書 - The 'Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature.'
  • 陽道州 - Yang Daozhou, a historical figure.
  • 費縣 - Feixian, a county in Shandong province.
  • 白露 - The White Dew solar term in the Chinese calendar.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 李忠公之为相也 - Formal opening introducing Li Zhonggong as chancellor.
  • 政事堂有防食之床 - Literal description of a bed for guarding food in the Hall of Government Affairs; somewhat archaic.
  • 宰臣罢不迁者五十年 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'the chancellor was not moved' or 'the chancellor was not transferred.'
  • 朝夕论道之所 - Formal phrase referring to a place for discussing state affairs day and night.
  • 岂可使朽蠧之物而不除 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'how can one allow rotten and worm-eaten things to remain without removing them.'
  • 俗言拘忌何足聴也 - Formal phrase referring to superstitious sayings and taboos, which are not worth listening to.
  • 以此获免余之愿焉 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'I was able to escape this and achieve my wish.'
  • 命彻而焚其下铲去聚壤十四畚 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'he ordered it to be removed and burned, and the accumulated earth below was cleared away in fourteen baskets.'
  • 议者称正焉 - Formal phrase referring to those who discussed it praising it as correct.
  • 杜太保宣简公大厯中有故人遗黄金百两 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'Du Taibao, the Simple Duke, in the Dali era, had an old friend who left him a hundred pounds of gold.'
  • 后三十年为淮南节度使其子投公取其黄金还缄封如故 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'thirty years later, he became the Military Governor of Huainan, and his son came to him to retrieve the gold, which was still sealed as before.'
  • 赵郡三祖元和中每房一人同时为相皆第三即司徒吉甫司空绛华州刺史藩 - Elliptical phrase; could mean 'in the Yuanhe era of the Zhaojun Three Ancestors, one person from each branch served as chancellor at the same time, all of whom were the third in line, namely, the Grand Tutor Jifu, the Grand Works Manager Jiang, and the Governor of Huazhou Fan.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李忠公 - Refers to Li Zhonggong, a historical figure who served as chancellor.
  • 政事堂 - Refers to the Hall of Government Affairs, a place where high-ranking officials discussed state affairs.
  • 防食之床 - Refers to a bed used for guarding food, which is somewhat archaic and requires careful interpretation.
  • 宰臣 - Refers to the chancellor, the highest-ranking official in the government.
  • 朝夕论道之所 - Refers to a place where one discusses state affairs day and night, which is somewhat formal and requires careful interpretation.
  • 俗言拘忌 - Refers to superstitious sayings and taboos, which are not worth listening to.
  • 杜太保宣简公 - Refers to Du Taibao, the Simple Duke, a historical figure.
  • 大厯 - Refers to the Dali era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 淮南节度使 - Refers to the Military Governor of Huainan, a high-ranking military official.
  • 赵郡三祖 - Refers to the Zhaojun Three Ancestors, a group of historical figures.
  • 元和 - Refers to the Yuanhe era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 司徒 - Refers to the Grand Tutor, a high-ranking official.
  • 司空 - Refers to the Grand Works Manager, a high-ranking official.
  • 华州刺史 - Refers to the Governor of Huazhou, a high-ranking official.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 主丧事专诣开封诘金之所 - 主丧事 means 'to handle funeral affairs,' 专诣 means 'to specifically go to,' and 诘金之所 means 'to inquire about the place where the gold came from.' This phrase describes a specific task related to funeral matters.
  • 公请假至墓所以出金付之焉 - 公请假 means 'the official took leave,' 至墓所 means 'to the tomb,' and 出金付之焉 means 'took out the gold and handed it over.' This describes the official's actions after taking leave.
  • 韦献公夏卿有知人之鉴人不知也 - 韦献公夏卿 refers to Wei Xiangong Xia Qing, 有知人之鉴 means 'has the ability to discern people,' and 人不知也 means 'no one knows this.' This highlights Xia Qing's ability to recognize talent, which is unknown to others.
  • 因退朝于街中逄再从弟执谊从弟渠牟升三人皆第二十四并为郎官簇马良久 - 因退朝 means 'after dismissing from court,' 于街中逄 means 'met in the street,' 再从弟 means 'second cousin,' 执谊从弟渠牟升 refers to three individuals, and 皆第二十四并为郎官 means 'all were the twenty-fourth rank and served as officials.' 簇马良久 means 'gathered around their horses for a long time.' This describes a meeting among officials after court.
  • 献公曰今日逄三二十四郎輙欲题目之语执谊曰汝必为宰相善保其末耳语渠牟曰弟当别奉主上恩而速贵为公卿语升曰三人之中弟最长远而位极旄后竟如其言 - 献公曰 means 'Xia Qing said,' 今日逄三二十四郎 means 'today we met three officials of the twenty-fourth rank,' 輙欲题目之语 means 'let's give them titles,' 执谊曰 means 'Zhiyi said,' 汝必为宰相 means 'you will definitely become a prime minister,' 善保其末耳 means 'take good care of your position,' 语渠牟曰 means 'told Qu Mou,' 弟当别奉主上恩而速贵为公卿 means 'you will receive special favor and quickly become a high-ranking official,' 语升曰 means 'told Sheng,' 三人之中弟最长远而位极旄 means 'among the three, you will have the longest career and reach the highest rank,' 后竟如其言 means 'later, it turned out as he said.' This describes Xia Qing's predictions about the future careers of the three officials.
  • 杜亚为淮南竞渡采莲龙舟锦缆绣防之戏费金数千万于頔为襄州防山灯一上油二千石李昌防为荆南打猎大修富饰其妻独孤氏亦出女队二千人皆着红紫锦绣袄子此三府亦因而空耗 - 杜亚为淮南 means 'Du Ya was in Huainan,' 竞渡采莲龙舟锦缆绣防之戏 means 'dragon boat races, lotus picking, and other elaborate games,' 费金数千万 means 'cost millions of coins,' 于頔为襄州 means 'Yu Di was in Xiangzhou,' 防山灯一上油二千石 means 'lit mountain lanterns that consumed two thousand stones of oil,' 李昌防为荆南 means 'Li Changfang was in Jingnan,' 打猎大修富饰 means 'went hunting and spent lavishly on decorations,' 其妻独孤氏亦出女队二千人 means 'his wife Dugu also had a female troupe of two thousand people,' 皆着红紫锦绣袄子 means 'all wearing red and purple embroidered coats,' 此三府亦因而空耗 means 'these three regions also suffered from such extravagance.' This describes the lavish spending of officials in different regions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 韦献公夏卿 - Wei Xiangong Xia Qing was a Tang dynasty official known for his ability to discern talent.
  • 杜亚 - Du Ya was a Tang dynasty official who served in Huainan.
  • 于頔 - Yu Di was a Tang dynasty official who served in Xiangzhou.
  • 李昌防 - Li Changfang was a Tang dynasty official who served in Jingnan.
  • 独孤氏 - Dugu was the wife of Li Changfang.
  • 娄师徳 - Lou Shide was a Tang dynasty official known for his integrity and loyalty.
  • 兴元元年 - The first year of the Xingyuan era (880-881 CE).
  • 贞元十二年 - The twelfth year of the Zhenyuan era (786-805 CE).
  • 神防军 - The Divine Protection Army, a military force in the Tang dynasty.
  • 神武中防军 - The Divine Martial Army, a military force in the Tang dynasty.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建中初 - Refers to the early years of the Jianzhong era, a period during the Tang dynasty.
  • 诸司胥吏为 - Refers to various departments and low-ranking clerks or officials causing harm or trouble.
  • 颇多播议用士人掌之 - Means many of Guān Bō's proposals to use literati to manage them.
  • 承四太尉之后 - Refers to inheriting the legacy of four high-ranking military officials.
  • 世传黄雀所衔玉环 - Means passed down through generations about a yellow bird holding a jade ring.
  • 今不知所在 - Means now it is unknown where it is.
  • 自幽州入觐 - Means from Yōu zhōu to pay a formal visit to the emperor.
  • 见令李杭申桑梓之礼 - Means to see the magistrate and express filial piety.
  • 张公曰困危之中岂能相救 - Refers to Zhang Shougui saying in a difficult situation how can he help you.
  • 张茂宗所尚义章公主赠郑国公主谥为庄穆 - Refers to Zhang Mào zōng's daughter, Yì zhāng gōng zhǔ, being bestowed the title of Princess of Zheng and posthumously honored as Zhuāng Mù.
  • 韦宥所尚故唐安公主赠韩国公主谥为贞穆 - Refers to Wéi Yòu's daughter, Gù Táng ān gōng zhǔ, being bestowed the title of Princess of Hàn and posthumously honored as Zhēn Mù.
  • 所司择日册命 - Means the relevant authorities choosing a date to confer a title.
  • 公主追谥自此始也 - Means posthumous titles for princesses started from this point onward.
  • 徐家肺沈家脾真安穏 - Idiomatic expressions referring to the peace and stability of the families of Xú and Shěn.
  • 一日忽梦其物故拘至地府酆都狱 - Means one day, a scholar suddenly dreamed of being arrested and taken to the underworld due to his death.
  • 有牛头在旁其人了无畏惮 - Means there was a bull's head beside him, and he had no fear.
  • 仍以手抚其头云只者头子大堪熝食 - Means still touching the head and saying it was very suitable for boiling to eat.

Proper Nouns

  • 关播 - Historical figure, Guān Bō.
  • 给事中 - High-ranking official in the Tang court.
  • 杨氏 - Refers to the Yang family.
  • 四太尉 - Refers to four high-ranking military officials.
  • 黄雀 - Yellow bird.
  • 杨国忠 - Historical figure, Yáng Guózhōng.
  • 张守珪 - Historical figure, Zhāng Shǒuguī.
  • 平陆 - Place name, Píng lù.
  • 幽州 - Place name, Yōu zhōu.
  • 李杭 - Proper noun, Lǐ Háng.
  • 李冕 - Proper noun, Lǐ Miǎn.
  • 张公 - Refers to Zhang Shougui.
  • 兖州判官 - Judge in Yanzhou.
  • 贞元中 - Era name, Zhēnyuán zhōng.
  • 张茂宗 - Proper noun, Zhāng Mào zōng.
  • 义章公主 - Proper noun, Yì zhāng gōng zhǔ.
  • 郑国公主 - Title, Princess of Zheng.
  • 庄穆 - Posthumous title, Zhuāng Mù.
  • 韦宥 - Proper noun, Wéi Yòu.
  • 唐安公主 - Proper noun, Gù Táng ān gōng zhǔ.
  • 韩国公主 - Title, Princess of Hàn.
  • 贞穆 - Posthumous title, Zhēn Mù.
  • 徐尚书 - Proper noun, Xú shàng shū.
  • 沈吏部 - Proper noun, Shěn lì bù.
  • 杨东川 - Proper noun, Yáng dōng chuān.
  • 嗣复 - Descendant, sì fù.
  • 徐家肺 - Idiomatic expression referring to the family of Xú.
  • 沈家脾 - Idiomatic expression referring to the family of Shěn.
  • 熝牛头 - Boiled beef head.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 禁未仕不得乘大马 - Prohibition on unmarried officials riding large horses, emphasizing the strictness of the regulation.
  • 货不得举人不得骑 - Situation where the prohibition is so strict that even those with the means cannot ride, highlighting the severity of the regulation.
  • 当尽为河北节制所得耳 - Suggests that the prohibition is so severe that even the powerful military governors in the region might benefit from it, indicating a broader impact.
  • 鬻茶邸 - Refers to tea shops or tea houses, which were important social and economic institutions.
  • 烧瓦瓷为其形貌置于灶釜上左右为茶神 - Describes the practice of creating clay figures of tea gods and placing them near stoves or pots, highlighting the religious and cultural aspects of tea consumption.
  • 有交易则茶祭之无则以釡汤沃之 - Describes the ritual of offering tea to the tea gods during transactions and using hot water to honor them otherwise, emphasizing the spiritual and social dimensions of tea culture.
  • 高祖之制凡出将赐旌节专征行军有大总管之号也 - Describes the practice of granting military leaders the title of 'Grand Commander' and bestowing them with a ceremonial staff for special missions, highlighting the military and political structure of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 高祖之制镇军有大都督之号 - Describes the practice of granting military leaders the title of 'Grand Marshal' for defensive purposes, emphasizing the military hierarchy.
  • 开元九年命宗正寺官寮并以宗子为之 - Describes the decree in the ninth year of Emperor Xuanzong's reign that all officials in the Imperial Clan Bureau were to be appointed from among the royal family members, emphasizing the importance of the royal family in governance.
  • 唐制男子始生为黄四岁为小十六为中二十为丁六十为老赋役之制有四一曰租二曰税三曰役四曰徭凡丁岁输粟二石凡调随乡土所产绢绫絁各二丈布加五分之一麻三斤凡役岁二旬闰加二日 - Describes the Tang Dynasty's household registration system and the four types of taxes and labor services, specifying the amounts of grain, silk, and other goods required from adult males, emphasizing the economic and social structure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞元中 - A specific period during the reign of Emperor Dezong of the Tang Dynasty (785–805 AD).
  • 高祖 - Refers to Emperor Gaozu (Li Yuan), the founder of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 节度使 - Military governors who had significant power and influence during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 宗正寺 - The Imperial Clan Bureau, responsible for managing the royal family.
  • 宗子 - Members of the royal family.
  • 明经 - One of the highest-level civil service examinations in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 侍御史 - A high-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty, often involved in judicial and administrative duties.
  • 宣谕使 - A special envoy appointed to convey imperial decrees and handle specific tasks.
  • 僧及道士 - Monks and Taoist priests, who were often involved in religious and social activities.
  • 铸钱使 - The official responsible for coinage and monetary policy.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乾元二年 - Specific year in the Tang Dynasty's reign calendar, marking the time period of the events described.
  • 御史中丞 - High-ranking official in the Tang Dynasty's administrative hierarchy.
  • 江淮五道租庸使 - Title indicating the official's responsibility for tax collection in the Jianghuai region.
  • 高户定数 - Practice of setting a fixed quota for high-ranking households.
  • 白着 - Type of tax levied on high-ranking households.
  • 酤 - Refers to the sale or purchase of alcohol.
  • 至徳二年勅 - Specific imperial edict issued in the year Zhude 2.
  • 量定酤酒 - Means to set a quota for the sale of alcohol.
  • 随月纳税 - Indicates the practice of paying taxes on a monthly basis.
  • 建中二年 - Another specific year in the Tang Dynasty's reign calendar.
  • 青苖 - Refers to a type of early spring tax.
  • 大厯中 - Period during the Tang Dynasty's reign calendar.
  • 初税 - Refers to the initial tax rate.
  • 勅以御史大夫充使 - Means the emperor appointed a high-ranking official to carry out a mission.
  • 割归度支 - Indicates the transfer of responsibilities to the Ministry of Finance.
  • 卢中丞迈 - Specific official, likely a Middle Censor, who held the title '迈'.
  • 寒玉古磬 - Describes a type of ancient stone chime made of cold jade.
  • 响泉和志之号 - Refers to a title or name that combines the sounds of flowing water and harmonious intentions.
  • 李河南素 - Specific official, likely a Governor of Henan, who held the title '素'.
  • 杜公兼时 - Refers to a high-ranking official, likely a Grand Secretary, who held the title '时'.
  • 韩吏部愈 - Specific official, likely a Minister of Personnel, who held the title '愈'.

  • 乌江有项羽系乌骓树歴千余年尚郁茂 - Describes the historical and symbolic significance of the '乌骓' (Wuzhu) tree in the Wulin area, which has remained lush for over a thousand years.
  • 建中年中县令张勤厌宾客观逰令伐却 - Indicates that during the reign of Emperor Dezong (建中年), the county magistrate Zhang Qin was displeased with visitors admiring the tree and ordered it to be cut down.

  • 李镇恶即赵公峤之父选校梓州郪县令与友人书云 - Introduces a historical figure, Li Zhen'ao, who is the father of Zhao Gongqiao and served as the magistrate of Qizhou in Zizhou Prefecture.
  • 州带子号县带妻名由来不属老夫并是妇児官职 - A playful remark suggesting that the naming conventions of places (prefectures and counties) are not under the control of the speaker but rather seem to be influenced by the names of his family members.
  • 刘巨麟开元中为广州刺史弟仲丘为丽政殿学士兄弟友爱有罗浮道者为巨麟合丹剂将分半以遗仲丘命刀中破之分铢无差焉 - Describes the story of Liu Juling, who was the governor of Guangzhou during the Kaiyuan era, and his brother Zhongqiu, who was a scholar at the Lize Hall. They were close and shared a secret elixir, which was divided precisely.
  • 萧功曹頴士赵员外骥开元中同居兴敬里肄业共一靴久而见东郭之迹赵曰可谓驶于道路矣萧曰无乃禄在其中 - Chronicles the story of Xiang Gongcao Ying Shi and Zhao Yuwai Ji, who lived together in Xingjing Lane during the Kaiyuan era and shared a single pair of shoes, leading to a humorous exchange about their future prospects.
  • 苏州洞庭杭州兴徳寺房太尉琯云不逰兴徳洞庭未见山水 - Refers to the famous saying by Fang Taifu, who said that one cannot truly appreciate the beauty of the mountains and waters without visiting Xingde Temple and Dongting Lake in Suzhou and Hangzhou.
  • 寿安县有喷玉泉石溪皆山水之胜絶也贞元中李宾客洞为县令乃刬翳荟开径隧人方闻而异焉太和初博陵崔为主簿标堠于道周人方造而游焉 - Describes the natural beauty of Shou'an County, particularly the 'Pengyu Spring' and the stone stream, and how they became famous under the governance of Li Bingkong during the Zhenyuan era.

  • 凡代之移易者五六而公年九十所裹者犹幼小时様 - Means that despite changes in fashion over several generations, the style Pei wore at age ninety was the same as when he was young.
  • 今巾子有仆射様 - Indicates that the headband style Pei wore has become known as the 'Pei style.'
  • 韩太保皋生知音律尝观客弹琴为止息乃叹曰妙哉嵇生者也为是曲也 - Describes how Han Taibao recognized the music being played by a guest as the famous piece 'Guangling San' by Ji Kang.
  • 其当魏晋之际止息与广陵散同出而异者也 - Explains that the piece reflects the transition period between the Wei and Jin dynasties, sharing the same origin as 'Guangling San' but differing in some aspects.
  • 其音主商商为秋声天将肃杀草木摇落其岁之晏乎 - Describes the musical tone as associated with the autumn season, symbolizing the decline and end of the year.
  • 此所以为魏之季也 - Concludes that the music reflects the decline of the Wei dynasty.
  • 此所以知司马氏之将簒也 - Concludes that the music predicts the rise of the Sima family and their eventual usurpation of power.
  • 止息者晋虽兴终止息于此其音哀愤而噍杀操蹙而憯痛永嘉之乱其应乎此 - Describes the music as reflecting the sorrow and pain of the Jin dynasty's fall, particularly during the Yongjia Rebellion.
  • 叔夜撰此将贻后代知音且避晋祸托之鬼神史氏非知味者安得不传其谬也欤 - Suggests that Ji Kang composed the piece to pass it on to future generations and to avoid the political turmoil of his time by attributing it to supernatural beings.
  • 韩太保皋常言洪范五福独不言贵者近于高危福之自至犹儆动奈何枉道邀之 - Means that Han Taibao often said that among the five blessings in the *Great Plan* (洪范), wealth is the closest to danger, and one should not seek it through unethical means.
  • 李西平晟之将军也尝谒桑道茂云将军异日为京兆尹慎少杀人西平曰武夫岂有京兆尹望乎 - Describes how Li Xiping (李西平), a general, visited Sang Daomao (桑道茂) and was told he would become the governor of Jingzhao (京兆尹) in the future, but should be careful not to kill too many people. Li Xiping responded that a warrior would not have such aspirations.
